assertion_results.text: Step 0.3 reads the Last-Updated timestamp from the security-matrix.md HTML comment and parses the ISO 8601 value
assertion_results.evidence: Output displays parsed ISO 8601 timestamp '2026-05-01T10:00:00Z' (lines 13 and 28) and uses it to calculate 66 days since update, demonstrating successful reading and parsing of the Last-Updated value.

assertion_results.text: Step 0.3 detects that the matrix is older than the 14-day default threshold and displays a staleness warning
assertion_results.evidence: Output states 'Staleness threshold: 14 days' (line 8), computes 'Days since update: 66 days' (line 14), and displays a '> **WARNING**:' block (lines 16-21) for each stream, correctly detecting that 66 days exceeds the 14-day threshold.

assertion_results.text: The staleness warning includes the stream name, the last-updated date, and the number of days since the last update
assertion_results.evidence: Warning on line 16 includes stream name '2.1.x', last-updated date '2026-05-01', and days '66 days ago'. Second warning on line 30 includes stream name '2.2.x' with the same date and day count.

assertion_results.text: The warning presents three options: refresh now (re-run setup Step 10.6), proceed anyway, or stop
assertion_results.evidence: Lines 19-21 list three options: '1. Refresh now -- re-run matrix population (setup Step 10.6) for this stream', '2. Proceed anyway -- continue triage with the current matrix', '3. Stop -- halt triage so I can investigate'. All three required options are present with correct descriptions.

assertion_results.text: Step 0.3 runs before Step 0.5 (JIRA Access Initialization) — no Jira operations are attempted before the staleness check
assertion_results.evidence: The only output file is 'staleness-check.md' covering Step 0.3. Line 43 explicitly states: 'No Jira operations have been performed -- Step 0.3 runs before Step 0.5.' No Jira API calls or access tokens appear anywhere in the output.
